claim: Obsidian Mobile 1.12 (Feb 27 2026) added a native iOS Share extension to save content to the vault from other apps without opening Obsidian, plus a 'Bookmark Link' Shortcut action and a 'Unique note' app-icon long-press action.
quote: iOS: New Share extension to save content to your vault from other apps (e.g. Safari, social networks) without opening Obsidian. iOS: New Shortcut action 'Bookmark Link' to save URLs as Obsidian bookmarks.

claim: Obsidian Mobile 1.11 (Jan 2026) shipped Lock Screen, Control Center, and Home Screen widgets on iOS plus Android home-screen widgets, a Quick Settings Tile, and Siri/Shortcuts 'Capture to a note' that appends or prepends without launching the app.
quote: Lock Screen and Control Center widgets let you quickly Create a new note, Open a specific note, Open daily note, Open search, or Open Obsidian... New shortcuts: Open a note, Open daily note, Capture to a note. Siri: 'Capture using Obsidian'.

claim: Obsidian's public roadmap lists Mobile widgets, Siri/Shortcuts integration, Mobile UI refresh, and Mobile startup-time speed-ups as in-flight items, but no dedicated 'quick capture' entry remains beyond what shipped in 1.11/1.12.
quote: Mobile widgets — Quick actions from your lock screen, home screen, and more. Siri and Shortcuts integration — Support for opening and editing notes using Siri and Shortcuts iOS. Mobile startup time — Speed improvements for opening and editing notes on mobile.

claim: The 2026 Obsidian Report Card scores mobile 3.1/5 and notes the architectural problem that even widget/Shortcut taps still load the full vault, with users describing capture as 'still awkward and kludgy' despite 1.11/1.12 improvements.
quote: After you tap the widget icon, the full Obsidian app opens and loads your entire vault... 'Capturing to Obsidian on mobile is still awkward and kludgy'... 'Despite recent improvements to speed and quick capture, the friction is still to high'.

claim: Carlo Zottmann's Actions for Obsidian (iOS 18.4+, free) exposes 40+ dedicated Shortcuts actions — append, prepend, create from template, fetch frontmatter — making it a first-class building block for iOS Shortcuts/Action-button capture chains.
quote: Actions For Obsidian brings the power of 40+ dedicated Shortcuts actions to your iOS device, making Obsidian a first-class citizen in Apple's Shortcuts app.

claim: Quick Draft is a free Android/iOS companion app for Obsidian supporting text/voice/image capture with high-quality transcription, OCR, custom date-formatted file paths, template wrapping, share-sheet integration, and iOS Siri shortcuts; AI title generation is paid.
quote: Quick Draft is a free mobile companion app... Voice recording with high-quality transcription... Customizable file paths using date formatting (e.g., /YYYY/[W]-w/YYYY-MM-DD)... Widgets and iOS Siri shortcuts for rapid access.

claim: Drafts (Greg Pierce) since v28's Bookmarks feature can write Markdown files directly into an iCloud-stored Obsidian vault on iOS without opening Obsidian, making Drafts → Obsidian one of the most reliable lock-screen-fast capture flows.
quote: Thanks to the Bookmarks feature added in Drafts version 28, you can now store text outside of the Drafts iOS sandbox... the most reliable place to store a vault to be able to use file-based actions on iOS is in your iCloud Drive folder.

claim: Forum consensus on Android is that Tasker can drive Obsidian capture either via AutoInput/AutoShare plugins or — more reliably — by writing a timestamped Markdown file directly into the vault folder, with the caveat that sync only fires when Obsidian is opened.
quote: Just have Tasker write a file in the Vault folder of your choice!... the Vault sync only happens when Obsidian is opened, it seems.

claim: Harshil Chordia's open-source Android home-screen widget (github.com/harshilchordia/obsidian-widget, ~14 stars) renders pinned notes with interactive checkboxes and a Quick Capture button that appends to today's daily note without opening Obsidian.
quote: Pinned notes: show today's daily note or pick specific files. Interactive checkboxes — tap to toggle - [ ] / - [x]. Quick capture: Captures text and stores it as daily note in root folder.

claim: A 2025 quick-capture writeup recommends a two-tier flow: an iOS Shortcut widget that pops a system text box and appends date + #QuickNotes tag for short thoughts, falling back to Drafts for longer captures, explicitly to avoid Obsidian's slow cold-start.
quote: For a couple of sentences, this is fine by itself... Drafts is a rock solid app for quickly capturing Markdown text without losing it... it can take some time to open, and by that time, the thought we're trying to capture might have gone.

claim: Obsidian still has no first-party Apple Watch app in 2026; community workarounds rely on Drafts' Watch complication, Siri Shortcuts that round-trip via iCloud, or third-party watchOS recorders like Uplink that drop transcribed Markdown into the vault.
quote: Obsidian doesn't have a Watch app, but there are workarounds available... Uplink is a standalone watchOS recorder that turns spoken thought into Markdown notes in your own Obsidian vault.

claim: Fleeting Notes positions itself as the Android-first answer to Obsidian's mobile-capture friction, syncing scratch notes into the vault and frequently surfacing alongside Drafts and Funnel as Reddit/forum-recommended low-friction front-ends.
quote: Fleeting Notes... was developed specifically to fix the Quick Capture problem in Obsidian.
